text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful approach is to create SMART goals. This means each goal should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase page load speed by 20% within three months." It is also helpful to break down large goals into smaller milestones, such as completing research by a certain date. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved. When all team members and clients agree on the objectives, the project runs more smoothly.
